item.title: New York doctor who survived Ebola says he fears for healthcare workers
item.description: A New York doctor who contracted and survived Ebola more than a decade ago says he is worried for healthcare workers who are at the center of treating the latest outbreak.

item.title: Colorado governor commutes Trump ally Tina Peters' prison sentence
item.description: Colorado's governor on Friday announced he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters.

item.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
item.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday.

item.title: Israel, Lebanon extend ceasefire as Iran says it "cannot trust" U.S. in negotiations
item.description: Two days of talks in Washington between Lebanon and Israel produced an extension of the current ceasefire by 45 days.

item.title: Controversial FDA official leaving drug center post in latest departure at agency
item.description: Dr. Tracy Beth Høeg, leader of the Food and Drug Administration division responsible for regulating prescription and over-the-counter drugs, is leaving her post, a senior FDA official confirmed.

item.title: A longtime Atlanta principal retired, then returned to his school as a handyman
item.description: David White retired as the longtime principal at the Burgess-Peterson Academy in Atlanta, and then returned to the school as its handyman.

item.title: School principal retires, but returns as a handyman: "I'm contributing meaningfully"
item.description: 58-year-old David White retired after 33 years in education, the last 15 spent as an elementary school principal in Atlanta. But after "just hanging out with the cat," he decided to go back to school as a handyman. Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story.

item.title: Jerome Powell's legacy as he finishes Fed Chair term
item.description: Friday marked Jerome Powell's last day as Chairman of the Federal Reserve. CBS News producer Emily Pandise takes a look at his legacy.

item.title: What role does Raúl Castro play in Cuba as sources say the U.S. is moving to indict him
item.description: The U.S. is taking steps to indict Raúl Castro, the 94-year-old former president of Cuba, U.S. officials say. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: After a woman was released from ICE detention, her Army soldier husband speaks out
item.description: The wife of an active-duty U.S. Army soldier detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement was released on Thursday after a month in custody. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: 2 senators call on FAA to study impact of reduced flight attendant staffing
item.description: Sens. Tammy Duckworth​ of Illinois and Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin say their concern is there may be more emergency exit doors than flight attendants in the event of an evacuation.

item.title: 2 senators call on FAA to study safety impact of reduced flight attendant staffing
item.description: In a CBS News exclusive, a pair of Democratic Senators called on the Federal Aviation Administration to study the safety impact of allowing fewer flight attendants on some long-haul flights. CBS News transportation correspondent Kris Van Cleave has the latest.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for May 17, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, Taiwan's representative to the U.S. Alexander Yui and former Defense Secretary Robert Gates.

item.title: Trump talks up trade deals with China, but experts see no big wins for U.S.
item.description: President Trump's trip to China could bolster economic relations, but failed to deliver a breakthrough deal, some trade and energy experts said.

item.title: 1 firefighter dead, 10 other people injured in fire and explosion at lumber mill
item.description: Firefighters responded to a fire at Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, about 95 miles from Portland.

item.title: Health officials battle new Ebola outbreak: Doctor who survived virus speaks out
item.description: Doctors are monitoring an Ebola epidemic in Africa that may be to blame for dozens of deaths so far. Lilia Luciano reports and spoke with a U.S. doctor who recovered from the virus.

item.title: Terror plot targeting Jewish institutions foiled, suspect under arrest
item.description: A terror plot targeting Jewish institutions in New York, California and Arizona has been foiled, and a man linked to the alleged scheme is under arrest on Friday. Prosecutors say the Iraqi suspect is tied to other global attacks and claim he wanted payback for the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: 5/15: CBS Evening News
item.description: An Iraqi national was charged with plotting terror attacks in the U.S.; several were injured in a massive explosion at a Maine lumber mill.

item.title: Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow calls GOP rival a "formidable competitor"
item.description: The Democratic primary for the Michigan Senate race is heating up with less than three months to go before the election. Candidate and state Sen. Mallory McMorrow joins "The Takeout" to make her case for the upper chamber seat.

item.title: Rep. Ro Khanna says he's "concerned that Trump may be compromising" U.S. assurances to Taiwan
item.description: President Trump wrapped up a two-day summit in China with future arms sales to Taiwan in some doubt and scant details on future Chinese purchases of American jets and soybeans. Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna of California joins "The Takeout" with his reaction to the trip.

item.title: Cuba's biggest threat to U.S. is collapse, says former defense secretary
item.description: In an interview with "Face the Nation," Gates said another mass exodus from Cuba is the "biggest risk."

item.title: Pentagon's rush to counter drone threat may pose safety risks, specialist says
item.description: The safety specialist's warning appeared in a memo describing how a mini-drone had detonated and injured an Army Special Forces soldier.

item.title: CENTCOM chief says Iran's hold on strait has weakened, but threats remain
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper of U.S. Central Command told the Senate Armed Services Committee that U.S. forces have destroyed more than 90% of Iran's inventory of 8,000 naval mines.

item.title: Extended interview: Kacey Musgraves
item.description: In this web exclusive, Grammy-winning singer-songwriter Kacey Musgraves talks with correspondent Anthony Mason about her latest album, "Middle of Nowhere," a record inspired by loneliness following a breakup, and how she grew to feel empowered by the concept of liminal space.

item.title: Extended interview: Ayo Edebiri and Don Cheadle
item.description: In this web exclusive, actors Ayo Edebiri ("The Bear") and Don Cheadle ("Hotel Rwanda") talk with Tracy Smith about making their Broadway debuts in the revival of "Proof." They also discuss their love of live theater, and the early roles that inspired their passion for acting.

item.title: Extended interview: Sen. Dave McCormick on AI
item.description: In this web exclusive, Sen. Dave McCormick (R-Pa.) talks with Robert Costa about how energy and the rise of artificial intelligence go hand in hand; America's rivalry with China over AI; and nuclear power.

item.title: Extended interview: Scarlett Johansson
item.description: In this web exclusive, actress Scarlett Johansson talks about how dealing with her sensitive skin for years led to her founding a new line of skin care products, The Outset. She also discusses her life-changing roles, and the impact of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Extended Interview: Jean Smart
item.description: In this web exclusive, Jean Smart, the Emmy-winning star of "Hacks," talks with correspondent Tracy Smith about filming the final season of her HBO series.

item.title: Extended Interview: Stars of "Cats: The Jellicle Ball"
item.description: Mo Rocca sits down with Tony Award-winner André De Shields, Ken Ard, Junior LaBeija and Leiomy, who star in "Cats: The Jellicle Ball."

item.title: Extended Interview: Directors of "Cats: The Jellicle Ball"
item.description: Mo Rocca sits down with Zhailon Levingston and Bill Rauch, the directors of "Cats: The Jellicle Ball."
